cause: spec-judge flagged two apparatus-absorption findings (dir and JSON-path lifted verbatim from the design doc's worked examples) and one coverage gap (no-side-effect on bad name scenario'd only via worktree add); fixed by swapping in independent apparatus and adding a bare open --layout scenario before freeze

the conductor's mechanical apparatus swap at the spec gate rewrote the cwd scenario's Then but not its Given, leaving the two naming different JSON paths — a self-contradiction the impl-producer caught while building to it. An obvious stale-mistake contradiction (neither side plausibly intended), so served as a conductor minor fix rather than escalated as a Conflict floor: the Given was realigned to the Then. Lesson: a blind sed across a suite is not an apparatus swap; the swap must be read per scenario.

cold impl-judge returned change: all 46 frozen scenarios pass, but two undisclosed root-leaf defects — root-pane env silently dropped on both apply routes (contradicting the prose contract's native-env claim), and the worktree-add manifest misreporting the root pane's dir as cwd+dir when the pane sits at the bare worktree root. Fixed in code, never in the frozen suite. Conductor probed both backends live before briefing the fix rather than accepting the builder's degrade framing: env turns out to be native at the region tier on BOTH backends (herdr WorkspaceCreateParams/TabCreateParams carry env, verified against 0.7.4 CLI; tmux new-window takes -e), so the drop is properly fixable and was not degraded away.

the impl-producer emitted an --env flag on herdr's worktree create/open following what it took to be the schema's pattern, and honestly disclosed the spelling as unverified. Conductor verified against the live 0.7.4 socket schema and a throwaway-repo probe: WorktreeCreateParams and WorktreeOpenParams carry no env property at all, and the CLI answers 'unknown option: --env'. The flag would have thrown on the feature's primary flow (worktree add --layout) for any template whose root pane sets env. Removed; the root pane's env on that route now takes the design's own Gap C command-prefix fallback, which the design had recorded as having no customer and which this route turns out to be the first real customer for. Lesson: a producer's disclosed-unverified claim is a lead to close, not a caveat to ship.
